channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: I’m addicted to TikTok. I’m begging the government to ban it
item.description: I downloaded TikTok back in 2020, by which point it had moved from cringe teen fad to pandemic must-have. Since then, I’ve seen my attention span fracture and metastasize. The endless scroll subsumed me, along with some 48 million others in my Gen Z cohort.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: We’ve entered the AI grift era
item.description: For years, Y Combinator has been revered as a highly selective startup accelerator with a hit rate that far exceeds the law of averages. But one of its latest companies has raised a few eyebrows, and prompted questions about whether AI grifters have caught up with the innovators.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Scammers are flooding Trump’s new crypto exchange
item.description: Back in September, Donald Trump announced his new crypto exchange, World Liberty Financial (WLFI). Now, registered investors can officially sign up for the whitelist—assuming they can avoid the scammers.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: De la ‘sala de máquinas’ a la junta directiva: el nuevo rol del CIO, a debate en el CIO Summit 2024
item.description: Los debates marcaron, una vez más, gran parte de la agenda de la última edición de CIO Summit 2024, celebrado en Madrid los pasados 25 y 26 de septiembre.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Mystery surge in California tax revenue points to tech companies like Nvidia
item.description: California was nearly $2 billion over forecast in corporate tax receipts this summer, including by $844 million in July.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: Austrian activist Schrems wins privacy case against Meta over personal data on sexual orientation
item.description: The European Union’s top court has said that social media company Meta can’t use public information about a user’s sexual orientation obtained outside its platforms for personalized advertising under the bloc’s strict data privacy rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Why airlines are turning to Starlink for in-flight Wi-Fi
item.description: Last week, Air France was the latest major airline to announce it will be moving its in-flight Wi-Fi to the Starlink network, offering it for free to members of its frequent flier program.
item.dc:creator: Jack Kuhr, Fast Company

item.title: Google Threatens to Cut NZ News Ties Amid Revenue Laws
item.description: Google warns it will stop linking to New Zealand news sites and reverse support for local media if a law forcing tech companies to pay publishers passes
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.94 boosts file finding
item.description: The September 2024 release of Microsoft’s Visual Studio Code editor, version 1.94, features improvements for finding files using the File Explorer. The upgrade also introduces the ability to run Python tests with coverage.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Understanding VBS Enclaves, Windows’ new security technology
item.description: Recently Microsoft announced efforts to improve security in its planned Recall AI assistant tool. Many of the details were not a surprise, as it built on familiar tools and services I’ve written about during the past few years, but the most important feature, VBS Enclaves, will be new to most of us.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How Uber and Lyft are funding opposition efforts over San Francisco transit ballot
item.description: Uber and Lyft are funding opposition efforts over a ballot initiative in San Francisco that’s meant to increase funding for the city’s public transit system.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Spider lovers scurry to Colorado town in search of mating tarantulas and community
item.description: It’s tarantula mating season on the Colorado plains, when male spiders scurry out of their burrows in search of a mate.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert And Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: OpenAI previews Realtime API for speech-to-speech apps
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a public beta of the Realtime API, an API that allows paid developers to build low-latency, multi-modal experiences including text and speech in apps.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta and Australia have partnered to crack down on ‘celeb bait’ scam ads
item.description: Meta said it has taken down some 8,000 so-called “celeb bait” scam ads from Facebook and Instagram as part of a new effort with Australian banks to curb the practice.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Snapchat’s failure to protect kids revealed in New Mexico lawsuit
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico’s attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Apple Accused of Violating Union Rights: NLRB Complaint Filed
item.description: US National Labor Relations Board accuses Apple of violating union rights, coercively interrogating employees and interfering with their right to organize
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Women of Color Congressional Candidates Face Rampant Hate Speech on Social Media: Study Finds
item.description: A recent study reveals women of color congressional candidates are disproportionately targeted with hate speech and online abuse on social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information
item.description: The IG said CISA made some good improvements to the Automated Indicator Sharing program in recent years. The post CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: What lies beneath the Great Lakes' waves is largely unknown, but there's a new push to learn more about thousands of shipwrecks, underwater infrastructure and the impacts of climate change on the bottom of the world's largest freshwater system.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: WWW creator Tim Berners-Lee closes down his foundation
item.description: The World Wide Web Foundation, the organization whose mission has been to make the web safer and more accessible, has shut down, according to The Register. The foundation, which close its virtual doors Sept.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Appeals court reinstates Indiana lawsuit against TikTok alleging child safety, privacy concerns
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — The Indiana Court of Appeals has reinstated a lawsuit filed by the state accusing TikTok of deceiving its users about the video-sharing platform's level of inappropriate content for children and the security of its consumers’ personal information.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cruise fined $1.5M over robotaxi crash reporting
item.description: General Motors's autonomous vehicle unit, Cruise, will pay a $1.5 million fine after it failed to disclose details initially about a serious crash involving a pedestrian last year, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Device42 amps up IT asset management with generative AI
item.description: Device42 today announced new functionality in its IT inventory and asset management virtual appliance that enables IT managers to apply generative AI to query their configuration management database (CMDB) using natural language and more quickly get responses via a chat-like interface.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Verizon, AT&T, T-Mobile service outages reported by users
item.description: People in multiple cities posted about issues they were having, with many saying their phones were in "SOS" mode.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Cruise ship surgery that slices giants for mega profits
item.description: A whopping 30 million people will have taken cruises in 2024, and some ships being built to keep up with this demand cost as much as $2 billion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazil judge makes new requests to allow X to be reinstated from suspension
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes on Friday added conditions for Elon Musk’s X to have its service reestablished in the country, one day after the social media platform said it had complied with all the judge’s demands, including naming a legal representative.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, NewsNation
